notes.note: A young patrician of the Claudii Pulchri whom Cicero commends to Decimus Brutus; the filiation "son of Gaius" (Latin C. f.) is the standard Roman way of identifying a man by his father's first name.

Clementia, mercy toward a defeated or compromised opponent, had become a prized public virtue after Caesar's much-advertised clemency; Cicero urges Decimus Brutus to add it to his reputation for courage.

notes.anchor: it was filial duty that brought him into Antony's camp
notes.note: Pietas, the duty owed to family, gods, and country, is offered as the excuse for the young man's siding with Antony: he did it to secure the recall of his exiled father.
